issues.description: The Playwright webServer command in e2e/playwright.config.ts uses a hardcoded absolute path (/home/dinesh-se/Dev/personal-website) that only exists on the developer's machine. In CI (GitHub Actions runners), this path doesn't exist, so the shell command fails silently — the Next.js dev server never starts. Playwright then waits for port 3099 to be available (up to its 60s timeout), and when tests finally run, they get ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED or ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE because nothing is listening on that port. All 47 failing e2e tests share this single cause.
issues.source:
e2e spec "should render the page title" (home.spec.ts) failed: Error: page.goto: net::ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE at http://localhost:3099/
Call log:
[2m  - navigating to "http://localhost:3099/", waiting until "load"[22m
